application.name: Anky
application.targetHuman: A person who reaches for a feed before hearing their own uncensored thought.
application.coreAction: Write forward continuously before scrolling so one honest train of thought can be completed and remembered.

constraints: Capture forward graphemes without paste or newline.
Preserve the frozen .anky v0 byte format through a compatibility adapter.
Do not make editing or polishing the purpose of the action.

feedback.onProgress: Keep the writing field quiet while progress is felt through the ritual rather than a dashboard.
feedback.onCompletion: Acknowledge eight active minutes without ending or publishing the writing.
feedback.onInterruption: Seal the current bytes as a private complete or interrupted event and offer the next honest action.

runtime.returnInvitation: Invite another private writing session at the person's chosen rhythm, including the optional write-before-scroll gate.

accumulated.description: Private sessions accumulate as practiced time, days, reflections, and an Anky painting journey without turning the practice into a public score.

externalDisclosure: Exact private artifact only after the owner requests a reflection for that event.
The configured reflection service and provider receive only the fields declared by the selected policy.

forbiddenPatterns: profile or account form before first value
social feed or public writing by default
artifact hash used as stable event identity
visualDirection: progress communicated without a productivity dashboard
implementationNotes: Keep .anky v0 and anky.base.eoa.v1 behind compatibility boundaries; do not rename them into generic TOHSENO primitives.
Give each event a stable ID independent of every artifact digest.
Do not mutate a sealed artifact to prepare reflection; create a derived artifact with an explicit relation if normalization is necessary.
Treat fragment reflection policy, generic identity cryptography, Android recovery, and application-level encryption as unresolved decisions rather than implied guarantees.

operatorNotes: This manifest records a compatibility target; it does not assert current iOS and Android runtime parity.
Reflection service failures must never invalidate a locally committed event.
